chapter_title.en: Chapter 81 – The Talbiyya (The exclamation: ‘Here I am Lord<sup>azwj</sup>! Here I am!)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: كالموثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/268)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: كالموثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/268)
text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) مَنْ لَبَّى فِي إِحْرَامِهِ سَبْعِينَ مَرَّةً إِيمَاناً وَ احْتِسَاباً أَشْهَدَ اللَّهُ لَهُ أَلْفَ أَلْفِ مَلَكٍ بِبَرَاءَةٍ مِنَ النَّارِ وَ بَرَاءَةٍ مِنَ النِّفَاقِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Abu Abdullah, from Ibn Fazzal, from various men,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’farasws having said: ‘Rasool-Allahsaww said: ‘The one who exclaims Talbiyya seventy times being in his Ihraam with having Eman (faith) and considering it as a good deed, Allahazwj would Cause a thousand thousand Angels to testify for him with the freedom from the Fire, and freedom from the hypocrisy’.
en.sarwar: 8. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn abu ‘Abd Allah from ibn Faddal from many men who have said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, has stated this Hadith. ‘Whoever says Talbiyah seventy times out of belief and considering it a good deed Allah makes a million angels to bear witness to his freedom from the fire and hypocrisy.’”
